key_insight: The paper verifies a sequential, non-iterative fixed-stress thermo-poroelastic coupling in PFLOTRAN by showing that pressure, temperature, and deformation responses match benchmark solutions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a validated THM modeling foundation for predicting coupled heat, fluid, and stress effects in subsurface systems such as geothermal reservoirs and energy storage sites.
method: Solved TH mass and energy balances on control-volume/Voronoi cells
Solved quasi-static mechanics on an element-based dual mesh
Used a strictly sequential fixed-stress split: TH solve first, then mechanics update
evidence_signal: The implementation was evaluated against analytical and semi-analytical benchmarks for poroelastic and thermo-poroelastic cases, including pressure diffusion, temperature evolution, deformation, and a discontinuity/fracture mapping test.
watch_item: Because the coupling is strictly sequential and non-iterative, accuracy and robustness may be more sensitive in strongly coupled or highly discontinuous scenarios than in the benchmark cases.
paper_title: Verification of a sequential thermo-poroelasticity formulation in PFLOTRAN
